document.title: Technical Construction File For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

sourceSummary.oneLine: Technical Construction File TCF-001 Rev.5 for H&abyz HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG digital flat panel X-ray detectors under CE MDD documentation.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports CE mark documentation for a digital flat panel X-ray detector intended to move freely within the European Community and be put into service for its intended purpose.
keyPoints: The document identifies HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG as digital flat panel X-ray detector models.
The device is classified under MDD Annex IX Rule 16 as Class IIa.
The intended use is general radiographic imaging of human anatomy, replacing film or screen based radiographic systems, not for mammography.
The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd. and the EU representative is JaviTech e.K.

The file includes applicable standards, essential requirements, product description, materials, manufacturing and QC procedures, labeling, risk management, software validation, and clinical evaluation.

evidence.claim: The document is Technical File TCF-001 Rev.5 dated Dec/10/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical File Doc. No. TCF-001 Rev. No. 5 Rev. Date Dec/10/2020

evidence.claim: The Technical Construction File is for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Construction File For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co., Ltd. is the manufacturer.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer Company Name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: JaviTech e.K. is listed as the EU representative.
evidence.evidenceText: EU Representative: Company Name: JaviTech e.K.

evidence.claim: The file is intended to support CE marking and free movement within the European Community.
evidence.evidenceText: requested to bear the CE mark to enable them to move freely within the European Community and to put into service in accordance with its intended purposes

evidence.claim: The applicable regulatory directive is Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC.
evidence.evidenceText: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC of 5 September 2007 concerning medical devices.

evidence.claim: The device is classified under MDD Annex IX Rule 16 as Class IIa.
evidence.evidenceText: According to Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC Annex IX: Rule 16, Class Ⅱa.

evidence.claim: Rule 16 applies to non-active devices intended for recording X-ray diagnostic images.
evidence.evidenceText: Non-active devices specifically intended for recording of X-ray diagnostic image are in Class Ⅱa.

evidence.claim: The product common name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product/Trade/Common/Usual Name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray detector

evidence.claim: The device has GMDN Code 34167 for solid state x-ray imager.
evidence.evidenceText: GMDN Code: 34167 Solid state x-ray imager (flat panel/digital imager)

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are intended for general radiographic systems for human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in general purpose diagnostic procedures.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The intended patient population includes all people but excludes infants or pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Age: Available all people (Not to be used for infant or pregnant women)

evidence.claim: The detector does not make direct contact with the patient.
evidence.evidenceText: The Digital Flat Panel x-ray detector does not make direct contact with patient

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC uses a CsI scintillator and HAD1717MG uses a GDX scintillator.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC: CsI Scintillator Detector HAD1717MG: GDX Scintillator Detector

evidence.claim: The detector rated voltage is AC 100-240 V, rated frequency 50/60 Hz, and maximum power consumption 80VA.
evidence.evidenceText: Rated voltage: AC (100-240) V~ Rated frequency: 50/60 Hz Electric power consumption: MAX 80VA

evidence.claim: The detector mechanical dimensions are 460 x 460 x 15.4 mm and typical weight is 3.75 kg.
evidence.evidenceText: Dimension 460 x 460 x 15.4 mm Weight Typ 3.75Kg

evidence.claim: The detector supports Line Trigger and Auto Exposure Detection trigger modes.
evidence.evidenceText: Trigger Mode Line(Manual) Trigger Auto Trigger (Auto Exposure Detection)

evidence.claim: Software listed for the product includes firmware HAD-MD-FW Version 1.0 and software HAD-MD-SW Version 1.0.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Firmware Model Name: HAD-MD-FW Version: 1.0 Software Model Name: HAD-MD-SW Version:1.0.0 OS: Windows 7 or more

evidence.claim: Risk management was conducted according to MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC and EN ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk management was conducted to investigate the potential risk factor and safety of centrifuge by H&abyz Co., Ltd. in accordance with MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC and EN ISO 14971.

evidence.claim: Software validation is conducted and documented by EN 62304:2006.
evidence.evidenceText: This software validation is conducted and documented by EN62304:2006.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation is conducted and documented by MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4.
evidence.evidenceText: This Clinical evaluation is conducted and documented by MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4 (Clinical Evaluation).

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation conclusion states the detector is substantially equivalent to a marketed predicate device and raises no new safety or performance issues.
evidence.evidenceText: we conclude that Digital flat panel X-ray detector is substantially equivalent to the marketed predicate device and do not raise any new issues of safety or performance.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text is CE/MDD-focused and cites Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC.
The text lists CE certificate symbol 0068 but does not explicitly identify the notified body name.
OCR/text quality contains apparent errors such as 'ISO14791', 'centrifuge' in risk management objective, and inconsistent document numbers for risk files.
The document includes a signatory date of 2019.09.21 for the Essential Requirements declaration, while the document revision date is Dec/10/2020.
